name: Fair Fight Action
subject: These four states are hotspots for gerrymandering:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FAIR FIGHT ACTION
body:
The redistricting process can open the door for extremists to further obstruct our democracy through racial gerrymandering. |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/71479040/432239648/1793776944?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20231012_e_redistricting1_v1s3&ms=bsc_emm_ffa_20231012_e_redistricting1_v1s3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMDBkYzM5MS01MzY5LWVlMTEtOTkzNy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VGXyF3QwldIEBzDcMsr4tLFdodwnoiS_XMs2ThcJ_BI=&emci=7af2938b-5369-ee11-9937-00224832eb73&emdi=f00dc391-5369-ee11-9937-00224832eb73&ceid=1037277)

| | Have you heard the pro-voter news out of Alabama and Florida? In both states, anti-voter extremists had tried to weaken Black political power by manipulating the congressional redistricting process — but courts have now struck down those efforts. **Let’s back up a little, talk about how we got here, and lay out what comes next.** States recently had to launch their once-a-decade process of redrawing district lines, also known as redistricting. And it opened the door for extremists to further obstruct democracy through **racial gerrymandering.** Gerrymandering is the process of drawing district boundaries, often in illogical ways, to intentionally advantage one political party. And it’s one of the many tactics extremists are using to dilute the voting power of Black, brown, and young voters. How? By packing certain voters into as few districts as possible. **Here’s what that looked like in four states — and here’s where those efforts stand:** **Alabama**
Twenty seven percent of Alabama’s population is Black, but the state only has one Black member of the U.S. House — representing the one district where Black voters are a majority. How did this happen? Well, the extremist governor and state legislature refused to fairly represent the Black population when they recently redrew their Congressional map. The U.S. Supreme Court has since ruled that their map was unconstitutional. Still, the state’s leaders openly defied a federal court order and tried to go ahead with their original plan. But in late September, the Supreme Court upheld a lower court’s decision to block that map — a victory for the pro-democracy movement. **Florida**
Governor Ron DeSantis fought for a congressional map designed to eliminate a historically Black district. Pro-voter groups took the plan to court. In early September, a state judge ruled against the new map, and a federal court started hearing the case in late September. **Georgia**
Extremist lawmakers enacted congressional and state legislative maps that were specifically designed to weaken Black voting power in particular districts. Black voters and our pro-voter partners filed a lawsuit to challenge these unfair maps, and the case was recently heard in federal court. **Ohio**
After two years of redrawing state legislative maps, the Ohio Redistricting Committee voted unanimously in late September to adopt new maps drawn along party lines that will heavily favor Republican extremists. There are three ongoing court cases that challenge the current gerrymandered maps — in part for how they weaken Black political power — and the Ohio Supreme Court has struck down five of the ORC’s proposed maps so far. We’ll keep an eye on these cases and continue pushing back against attempts to weaken Black political power. Make no mistake, gerrymandering is part of a larger national agenda to block representation for marginalized groups and push ballot access further out of reach. As extremists inevitably continue to design unfair maps, we’ll fight back even harder to ensure that eligible voters can make their voices heard. name: Replace Sinema
subject: Abortion rights are at stake
body:
Sinema is no ally to reproductive rights | | | Peter – If you hadn’t heard, anti-abortion zealot and MAGA extremist Kari Lake is in the race against Ruben Gallego for Sinema’s senate seat. **Lake has supported a ban on all abortions, without exception.** And Sinema’s presence in the race could possibly hand the seat right to her. **Gallego, however, has been a stalwart ally of women’s rights and reproductive health in Congress.** That’s why Reproductive Freedom for All (formerly NARAL Pro-Choice America) gave them their very first endorsement since changing their name. Kyrsten Sinema has long proclaimed herself to be pro-choice. But when push comes to shove, Sinema has undermined abortion rights over and over.
* First, she confirmed nearly 100 Trump judges, including some of who were anti-choice and opposed by Planned Parenthood.
* Then, when Trump’s Supreme Court overturned Roe v Wade, she sided with Republicans instead of restoring abortion rights.
* Now, she’s saying that the Pentagon and President Biden should compromise with Tommy Tuberville’s demands to ban abortion in the military.

**Actions speak louder than words. Since the U.S. Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade and the Dobbs Decision last summer, 22 states have banned or *severely* curtailed abortion rights – including 15 outright bans. Several more right-wing state legislatures have tried to ban it but were stopped by the courts.
In Arizona, the state’s Supreme Court now must decide whether an abortion ban put on the books in 1864 is still valid. In the meantime, the state still bans abortion after 15 weeks thanks to a law signed by the most recent Republican governor.
**Over and over, Kyrsten Sinema has been part of the problem, not part of the solution. She claims she believes in a woman’s right to choose, but actions speak louder than words.**
**[Please chip in so we can replace Kyrsten Sinema with a Senator who is *actually* pro-choice and will never betray women >>>](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953wifClq4dvE3aunp8QgRd2uubFEF9w53-6xWbLXouWtdnWITtUCYxzev4GSZJ4_cErKRAVyMqCeNjnkb1kEBWkileNuRSmMC2rPmR0O1UIT3WgYSFpotRyKdxzASrLRDwE3RsGmjQ7Dg0Rhh5AeWsSllxpka11uiJTn88EzI1fJgKTCRIsYzX5kIAgHOsEXClNX9I9ROafMBaq-LM7vn9qWcnIJLaBNf863_-edhBgPuXZmjMz6GYYZcWp_5gNmAICT_xDfBRVEto-KjqP9-8stpoaYjeOyfmaVUcnVfTI6NQ/40d/ldTV2fm3R6qn3POXrVhYuQ/h8/3f4uR_j0bRWALiYWztqJyCEq7vYW4TlL0veThA8TYp0)**

clean_body: Sinema is no ally to reproductive rights | | | Peter – If you hadn’t heard, anti-abortion zealot and MAGA extremist Kari Lake is in the race against Ruben Gallego for Sinema’s senate seat. Lake has supported a ban on all abortions, without exception. And Sinema’s presence in the race could possibly hand the seat right to her. Gallego, however, has been a stalwart ally of women’s rights and reproductive health in Congress. That’s why Reproductive Freedom for All (formerly NARAL Pro-Choice America) gave them their very first endorsement since changing their name. Kyrsten Sinema has long proclaimed herself to be pro-choice. But when push comes to shove, Sinema has undermined abortion rights over and over. First, she confirmed nearly 100 Trump judges, including some of who were anti-choice and opposed by Planned Parenthood. Then, when Trump’s Supreme Court overturned Roe v Wade, she sided with Republicans instead of restoring abortion rights. Now, she’s saying that the Pentagon and President Biden should compromise with Tommy Tuberville’s demands to ban abortion in the military. Actions speak louder than words. Sinema claims to be pro-choice, but time and again she’s stood in the way.**** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | | Since the U.S. Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade and the Dobbs Decision last summer, 22 states have banned or severely curtailed abortion rights – including 15 outright bans. Several more right-wing state legislatures have tried to ban it but were stopped by the courts. In Arizona, the state’s Supreme Court now must decide whether an abortion ban put on the books in 1864 is still valid. In the meantime, the state still bans abortion after 15 weeks thanks to a law signed by the most recent Republican governor. Over and over, Kyrsten Sinema has been part of the problem, not part of the solution. body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/embutton?refcode=X_101023_CORE_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/embutton?refcode=X_101023_CORE_act]
Hi friend,
Colorado is the most beautiful state in the country. And there’s no better way to see everything our home has to offer than long hikes in the summer.
When Michael was home, he did just that — bringing together mayors, advocates, and sportsmen on these hikes to talk about why these public lands are so critical to Colorado. Check out some of his pictures below!
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/embutton?refcode=X_101023_CORE_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/embutton?refcode=X_101023_CORE_act]
Summiting Quandary Peak with his daughter Halina
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/embutton?refcode=X_101023_CORE_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/embutton?refcode=X_101023_CORE_act]
Hiking the Mayflower Gulch trail
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/embutton?refcode=X_101023_CORE_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/embutton?refcode=X_101023_CORE_act]
On the trail around Loch Lake
One of Michael’s top priorities in the Senate has been protecting public lands across Colorado.
In 2022, he helped establish the Camp Hale-Continental Divide National Monument, which protected more than 50,000 acres of public land. The Tenmile Range — which includes Quandary Peak, one of the 14’ers Michael climbed this summer — is part of this protected land.
Michael has also been working hard to pass the Colorado Outdoor Recreation & Economy (CORE) Act, which would protect 400,000 acres of public lands. To draft this bill, Michael brought together local government, businesses, recreation groups, sportsmen, and conservationists. The end result is a win-win for our economy, our culture of outdoor recreation, and Colorado’s ecosystems.
Will you join a coalition of Coloradans showing their support for the CORE Act? Sign on now to help Michael protect our public lands >> [https://secure.michaelbennet.com/a/x_100423_core_act]
ADD YOUR NAME [https://secure.michaelbennet.com/a/x_100423_core_act]
ADD YOUR NAME [https://secure.michaelbennet.com/a/x_100423_core_act]
Happy hiking!
Thanks,
Team Bennet

| | Patriot,

---
Remember when I chose to go to Michigan to deliver a speech directly to the PEOPLE on how I’ll save our country rather than attend the GOP debate?
---
And remember when the so-called experts in Washington said I was making a big mistake?
---
Well, take a look at this new poll out of Michigan...
---
_**DETROIT NEWS: TRUMP NOW LEADS JOE BIDEN BY 7 POINTS IN MICHIGAN**
_
---
By now, I think we can safely say I made the RIGHT DECISION by saying “no thank you” to the offer to attend the last debate.

---
So, for everyone out there who is keeping score of how many times I’ve been right on so many different topics over the years, I think we’re currently sitting at a very high number.
---
But before the Fake News says, “There he goes again. Trump can’t stop bragging” – I want to make something clear... I don’t say any of this to brag.

---
The truth is: **_I am not alone in being right so often._**

---
I only say things that the vast (silent) MAJORITY of Americans agree with. And the majority of Americans have a lot more sense than all of the experts in Washington – COMBINED!

---
All decent and patriotic citizens agree that our borders should be secure, that a travel ban that restricts immigration from nations compromised by radical Islamic terrorism is a smart idea, that we should drill for oil on our own land, that we should pursue a foreign policy that promotes peace and stability, that we shouldn’t keep burning through taxpayer dollars when inflation is killing us, and that letting violent criminals out of jail is a dangerously stupid idea.

---
Right now, our country needs real leadership. We need someone who is unafraid to say what all Americans are thinking. We need someone with an independent mind who will ignore the Washington experts and do what’s right for our country. We need a leader who has the courage to defy the powers-that-be and implement a bold vision to Make America Great Again!

---
**_If you are ready for that leader, then I am humbly asking for your support to win back the White House and SAVE AMERICA._**

---

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: This honestly makes me want to tear my hair out
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=99%2E4032634%2Eou0gG0)
---
When I read headline after headline that says “Congress” is dysfunctional, I want to tear my hair out. And I want to grab a red pen, cross out “Congress,” and replace it with “The GOP-controlled House of Representatives.” Because that’s where the chaos is coming from. The way I see it, Congress as a whole is not dysfunctional. The problem is we have a handful of Trump loyalists that are determined to burn this place down, and a Republican Party that is enabling them. When Democrats had the House, Senate, and White House in the first two years of President Joe Biden’s term, we got things done. We passed a huge infrastructure bill that's paying off around the country as we speak. We passed the biggest climate package in the history of the world. We put in a 15% minimum corporate tax. We passed a $35 cap on insulin for seniors. The Biden-Harris administration has now delivered $127 billion in student debt relief — and is continuing to cancel debt for more and more borrowers. What have the Republicans done since taking the House? 1. They nearly defaulted on the national debt. 2. They nearly shut down the government. And 3. For the first time in history they deposed their own Speaker. Those are their three big accomplishments. Great job. What is the function of government, other than trying to make life better for people, to expand opportunity, and try to deal with the real problems that families have? That’s what Democrats are doing. Meanwhile, Republicans are hijacking our government, damaging our democracy in order to hurt more people and grab more power for themselves, and trying to make us so frustrated that we just tune out. But there’s no way I’m going to give them that satisfaction. And I hope you won't either. **[I’m going to keep doing everything in my power to stand up to these extremists and fight for the bold, systemic change we need to protect our democracy and our values. If you’re able to, please pitch in $28 or anything you can to our re-election campaign to help continue this fight.](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/812?amount=28&t=8&akid=99%2E4032634%2Eou0gG0)**
